Agent: Good morning, you've reached the Nigeria Police Force Public Relations Desk, this is Officer Nkechi speaking. For quality and training purposes, this call may be recorded. Do I have your consent to proceed?

Client: Yes, Officer Nkechi, you have my consent. Good morning.

Agent: Thank you, sir. How may I assist you today?

Client: My car was involved in a minor accident last week, and my insurance company is asking for a police report before they can process my claim. I just wanted to know the correct procedure to get one.

Agent: I understand, sir. Getting a police report for an insurance claim is a straightforward process. First, you will need to visit the nearest police station to where the incident occurred, or your local divisional police headquarters.

Client: Okay, the nearest station. Got it.

Agent: Yes. When you go, you should be prepared with the following documents and information: a valid means of identification, like your national ID card, driver's license, or international passport. You'll also need details of the incident, including the date, time, and location of the accident, and a brief description of what happened. If you have your insurance policy details, please take those along as well.

Client: So, my ID, details of the accident, and my insurance policy. Is there any form I need to fill?

Agent: Yes, at the station, you will be directed to the Desk Officer or the Divisional Crime Officer, who will provide you with a form to fill out, detailing the incident. You may also be required to write a formal statement. Once the statement is taken and the particulars are verified, the report will be prepared for you. There might be a processing fee involved, so please be prepared for that.

Client: A processing fee? How much is it usually?

Agent: The fee varies slightly by division, but it's typically a nominal amount for administrative processing. The Desk Officer will inform you of the exact amount. Once payment is confirmed, you will be issued the official police report. It usually takes a few hours to a day, depending on the station's workload.

Client: Alright, that's very clear. So, visit the station, bring ID, accident details, insurance info, fill a form, make a statement, pay a fee, and then collect the report. Thank you very much, Officer Nkechi. This was very helpful.

Agent: You are most welcome, sir. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

Client: No, that's all for now. Thanks again.

Agent: It was a pleasure assisting you. Have a good day.

Client: You too.

**Agent:** Good morning, Police Public Complaints Unit. My name is Ifeanyi. For quality assurance and training purposes, please note that this call is recorded. Do I have your consent to proceed?

**Client:** (Voice raised, agitated) Yes, yes, I consent! Just listen to what happened to me this morning! I want to report one of your officers, this is totally unacceptable!

**Agent:** I understand you're very upset, sir. I'm truly sorry you had such an experience. Please, can you tell me calmly what happened and where it occurred?

**Client:** Calmly? How can I be calm when I was just insulted by a police officer for no reason! I was driving along the Lekki-Epe Expressway, just after the Charly Boy bus stop, around 8:30 AM. There was a checkpoint there.

**Agent:** Okay, Lekki-Epe Expressway, after Charly Boy bus stop, around 8:30 AM. Please continue, sir.

**Client:** This officer, a tall, dark-skinned man, he had no name tag on his uniform, which is even worse! He just stopped my car, didn't even greet me properly. He just barked, "Where are your particulars?" I showed him everything – my driver's license, vehicle papers, everything was current. But he kept shaking his head, saying "This one is expired," pointing at my insurance which is clearly valid till next year! Then he started demanding I "settle" him, threatening to delay me. When I refused, he started shouting, saying I'm a rude civilian and I don't respect authority! It was so embarrassing!

**Agent:** I hear you, sir, and I truly apologize for the unprofessional conduct you've described. It's not acceptable. So, a tall, dark-skinned officer, no name tag, at a checkpoint after Charly Boy bus stop on Lekki-Epe Expressway, demanded a bribe and was verbally abusive after you refused. Is that correct?

**Client:** Exactly! He was very rude, speaking to me like I'm a criminal! I just want something done about him. This kind of behaviour gives the entire force a bad name.

**Agent:** I completely agree, sir. We take these matters very seriously. Can you recall any other distinguishing features about the officer or his uniform? Perhaps a specific unit patch or anything?

**Client:** I didn't see any patch. Just his regular police uniform, but like I said, no name tag. He was holding a short stick, you know, the black one.

**Agent:** Thank you for providing these details, sir. We will immediately escalate this complaint to the appropriate division for investigation. We do not condone such conduct from our officers. Your complaint has been logged under the reference number **PCRU-20240725-015**. Please keep this number safe for future reference. We may contact you if further information is required.

**Client:** (Slightly calmer) Okay, I have the number. I hope you people truly do something about it. Thank you.

**Agent:** We will, sir. Thank you for calling the Police Public Complaints Unit. Have a good day.

Client: It was around 10:30 AM this morning. The checkpoint on the road leading from Obalende to CMS. Agent: Thank you for those details, sir. Just to confirm, the location was the police checkpoint on the Obalende-CMS road, around 10:30 AM this morning. Client: Yes, exactly! It's just not right. We're citizens, not criminals.

Agent: You are absolutely right, sir. Every citizen deserves to be treated with respect. We take all complaints seriously, and this will be thoroughly investigated. Can I please get your full name and a contact number so we can reach you for further details or updates?

Client: My name is Mr. Segun Adebayo, and my number is 0803-123-4567.

Agent: Thank you, Mr. Adebayo. Your complaint has been logged, and we will commence an investigation immediately based on the information you've provided. Your reference ID for this complaint is PCRU-5789. Please keep this number safe for any future inquiries regarding this matter.

Client: Okay, thank you. I hope something is actually done about it.

Agent: We will ensure it is, Mr. Adebayo. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

Client: No, that's all. Thank you.

Agent: Thank you for contacting the Police Complaints Response Unit, Mr. Adebayo. Have a good day.

You may receive a call back within 48 hours for further discussion. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

Client: No, that would be all. Thank you very much for your prompt assistance.

**Client:** I don't know, I am hiding! No uniforms, just regular clothes. But they have big guns! Please, they are breaking into houses!

**Agent:** Sir, stay hidden and stay safe. Do not engage them. We have received your report. A rapid response team from the nearest division is being mobilized right now and will be dispatched to Tafawa Balewa village immediately. They are on their way. Can you give me your name and a contact number in case we need to reach you for more information?

**Client:** My name is Musa Abdullahi. My number is 080... (provides number). Please hurry!

**Agent:** Thank you, Mr. Abdullahi. Stay calm and stay hidden. Help is on the way. We will contact you if needed. Do you have any other information you can provide right now?

**Client:** No, just please, hurry! My family is here!

**Agent:** Understood. We are sending help now. Stay safe, Mr. Abdullahi. Ending call.

analysis.agent_note: Caller reported an ongoing bandit attack in Tafawa Balewa village, Kaduna State, involving 10-15 armed men on motorcycles. Rapid response team has been dispatched.
